target: 16x16 finite-grid covering existence at B=2000 (sharper than slot740's 8x8 at B=500)
rationale: Slot740 mechanism (greedy set cover + CRT realization of m, then native_decide) is proven scalable; question is whether the 16x16 finite question is satisfiable. Monte-Carlo over 1135-prime pool (B=10000, idx-1+2) caps at 0.751 coverage of K x L — strongly suggests 16x16 is uncoverable, in which case Aristotle gets a clean impossibility theorem. If 16x16 IS coverable, that's a strict strengthening of slot740. Either way produces a measurable advance over slot740, but neither resolves the open E203 conjecture itself (lift problem remains).
search_evidence: scripts/erdos203_lift_retry.py + analysis/erdos203_lift_retry_may30.json: no full quotient cover for B in {2000, 5000, 10000} (best 0.751 MC coverage).
